confidence_meta.reason: No catalyst within 48h (H.4.1 is 3 days out, FOMC 8 days out); MOVE below 80; scores not split 2-2. Note: RRP buffer at effectively zero is a rising tail risk not captured by this formula.

structured_conclusion.macro_trend: Restrictive-but-Easing at the Margin / Asia Equity Stress
structured_conclusion.likely_rotation: US semis & quality growth (SOXX, QQQ, DRAM outperforming); avoid Korea/Japan equities
structured_conclusion.korea_gate: 1/3 — USDKRW pass; EWY below SMA20 fail; KOSPI below 8,200 fail

bias: Financial conditions remain restrictive. The underlying plumbing (TGA, reserves) hasn't moved since Jul 19 — same H.4.1 vintage, next print Jul 23 — while the RRP buffer has drained to effectively zero and term yields are drifting higher into the Jul 28-29 FOMC.

counterargs.thesis: Liquidity plumbing is stable, not tightening further, despite curve steepening
supporting: WALCL, TGA, and reserves are unchanged since Jul 19 (same Jul 15 H.4.1 print) — no fresh drain
Bank reserves sit at $3.14T, above the $3T comfort line
VIX fell 4.7% to 17.88 — no equity vol stress
Breakevens (T5YIE, T10YIE) stable-to-lower despite oil's rise
Credit spreads (HY OAS) only marginally wider at 2.73%
against: US10Y and US30Y both rose today; curve bear-steepened (2s10s widened to 0.383%)
Oil up ~10% since Jul 8 to $82.06 — inflation risk building beneath calm breakevens
RRP buffer drained further to $0.03B (from $0.10B Jul 19) — no shock absorber left for the next TGA rebuild
KOSPI, NI225, Samsung, and SK Hynix all down ~4% today — Asia tech-equity stress
USDJPY still pinned above the 162 BOJ intervention threshold
MOVE index stuck near 71 (Friday's print, no new data yet) — still above pre-2026 calm norms
counterargs.read: The plumbing is holding steady rather than actively easing or tightening — the next real signal comes with the Jul 23 H.4.1 print. The RRP buffer sitting at effectively zero is the key vulnerability: the next TGA rebuild will hit reserves directly with no cushion left. Meanwhile the term structure is repricing higher into the Jul 28-29 FOMC — a real-rate-pressure setup, not a liquidity crisis. The sharp single-day selloff in Korean and Japanese equities looks idiosyncratic to Asia tech (Samsung, SK Hynix both -4.2/4.3%) rather than a broad funding-stress signal, since US semis (SOXX, DRAM) and domestic credit proxies are unaffected.

trade_table.rationale: SOXX +1.7%, DRAM +2.3% while reserves build and TGA drains; diverging positively from the Asia semis selloff.
Gate closed 1/3; KOSPI -4.5% and SK Hynix/Samsung both -4.2/4.3% today; EWY well below its 20-day SMA.
US10Y/30Y both rising into the Jul 28-29 FOMC; curve bear-steepening; TLT -0.78% today.
HY OAS marginally wider but ALLY/COF proxies firm; no real stress signal yet.
Pinned above the 162 BOJ threshold; NI225 -4% today warrants monitoring for intervention risk.

catalysts.note: Next weekly Fed balance sheet update
Key inflation print given oil's recent rise

plumbing.tga_delta: flat vs Jul 19 — same Jul 15 H.4.1 vintage, next release Jul 23 (down $163B from Jun 24 print)
plumbing.rrp_delta: drained further from $0.10B (Jul 19) — buffer effectively zero
plumbing.reserves_delta: flat vs Jul 19 — same H.4.1 vintage (up $192B from Jun 24 print, above $3T)

duration.move_delta: flat vs Jul 19 (Friday's print carried over)

duration_score.classification: Real-Rate Repricing / Bear Steepening (not CPI panic)
duration_score.reason: US10Y and US30Y both rose today while T5YIE/T10YIE breakevens held flat-to-lower and nominal US02Y stayed sticky at 4.217%. This reads as real yields rising on term-premium / Fed-higher-for-longer repricing into the Jul 28-29 FOMC, not a fresh inflation scare — DFII5 (2.01%) and DFII10 (2.32%) are the cleanest read. Kept only mildly negative because plumbing is holding steady (not actively tightening) and equity vol (VIX) is calm, even though the RRP buffer being at zero removes any cushion.

james_framework.what_market_IS_saying: Fed staying restrictive for longer into the FOMC; front-end sticky, long-end selling off on term premium — not a fresh inflation shock.
james_framework.pain_trade: Real-rate pressure on long duration (TLT -0.78%), not CPI panic — breakevens aren't moving.
james_framework.watch_signal: If nominal 2Y/10Y start falling into or after the Jul 28-29 FOMC → transition to cuts/growth-scare trade; watch RRP (already near zero) for signs a TGA rebuild starts draining reserves directly.

sector_rotation.current_regime: Mixed — easing plumbing vs. real-rate repricing

actionable_output.rotation_into: US semis, quality growth (SOXX, QQQ, DRAM)

sector_rotation.note: Signals are genuinely mixed this run: TGA/reserves flows point toward liquidity expansion while rising nominal yields and cumulative oil strength point toward inflation reacceleration. Neither regime fits cleanly — treat with low confidence.
